name: Kashi Vishwanath Temple

history.overview: Kashi Vishwanath Temple is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas dedicated to Lord Shiva and is one of the holiest temples in India.
history.built_by: The present temple was rebuilt by Queen Ahilyabai Holkar of Indore in 1780 after several destructions and reconstructions over the centuries.
history.architecture: Built in the traditional North Indian Nagara style with a magnificent gold-plated dome and spire.
history.importance: It is one of the most sacred pilgrimage sites for Hindus and is believed to grant liberation (Moksha) to devotees.

story: Welcome to the sacred Kashi Vishwanath Temple, one of the holiest shrines of Lord Shiva. Have you ever wondered why Kashi is called the City of Light? According to ancient tradition, Lord Shiva chose this sacred city as His eternal home and promised never to leave it. It is believed that those who visit Kashi with a pure heart receive His divine blessings, and those who spend their final moments here attain Moksha, freedom from the cycle of birth and death. At the heart of this temple lies the revered Jyotirlinga, symbolizing Shiva's infinite divine light. For countless devotees, this is more than a temple—it is a place where faith, devotion, and spirituality come together. May Lord Vishwanath bless you with peace, wisdom, and everlasting happiness.
seasonal_note: October to March is considered the best time to visit due to pleasant weather. During Maha Shivaratri and Dev Deepawali, the temple receives a huge number of devotees.
break_time: No fixed break. Darshan may be temporarily paused during special rituals.

mangala_aarti.description: Pratah aarti — sabse pehli aarti, bhakt bahut kam hote hain.

bhog_aarti.description: Dopahar ki aarti — bhog (prasad) chadhaya jaata hai.

sandhya_aarti.description: Sham ki aarti — bahut bheed hoti hai.

shringar_aarti.description: Shivling ka shringar kiya jaata hai.

shayan_aarti.description: Raat ki antim aarti — Shivji ko sulaya jaata hai.

best_visit_time: Early morning during Mangala Aarti (3:00 AM–5:00 AM) or evening during Sandhya Aarti (7:00 PM).
entry_fee: General entry is free. Special darshan tickets are available at additional cost.
dress_code: Traditional attire is preferred. Visitors should wear modest clothing with shoulders and knees covered.

name: Chausath Yogini Temple

history.overview: Chausath Yogini Temple is one of the oldest surviving Yogini temples in India. Located atop a hill at Bhedaghat near Jabalpur, it is renowned for its unique circular architecture and panoramic views of the Narmada River.
history.built_by: The temple was constructed during the 10th century by the Kalachuri dynasty. The central Gauri-Shankar shrine was later added by Queen Alhanadevi in 1155 CE.
history.architecture: Built primarily of granite in a circular, open-to-the-sky design, the temple originally housed 64 Yogini shrines surrounding a central shrine dedicated to Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati.
history.importance: It is one of the few surviving Yogini temples in India and holds immense religious, historical, and archaeological significance. The temple is associated with Shakta and Tantric traditions.

story: Welcome to the ancient Chausath Yogini Temple, a magnificent monument that has stood for over a thousand years. Dedicated to the sixty-four Yoginis, the divine manifestations of Adi Shakti, this temple represents the sacred feminine power of the universe. Built on a hill overlooking the holy Narmada River, the circular temple symbolizes the cosmic cycle of creation and destruction. At its center stands the beautiful Gauri-Shankar shrine, representing the eternal union of Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati. Walking through the circular corridor surrounded by Yogini shrines is believed to bring spiritual peace and divine blessings. Today, the temple remains one of India's finest examples of medieval architecture and sacred heritage.
seasonal_note: October to March is the best time to visit. During monsoon, Bhedaghat becomes exceptionally scenic, though heavy rains may occasionally affect access.

mangala_aarti.description: Morning prayers at the central Gauri-Shankar shrine.

bhog_aarti.description: Midday offering to Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening aarti performed at the central shrine.

shringar_aarti.description: Special decoration of the Shiva-Parvati idols.

best_visit_time: Early morning (7:00 AM–9:00 AM) or evening before sunset for panoramic views of the Narmada River.
entry_fee: Entry is free for all visitors.
dress_code: Modest and respectful attire is recommended.

name: Maa Bal Kumari Temple

history.overview: Maa Bal Kumari Temple is a revered Siddh Peeth located near Neelkanth Mahadev in the hills of Rishikesh. Dedicated to Maa Parvati in her maiden form, the temple is known for its peaceful surroundings and panoramic Himalayan views.
history.built_by: According to local tradition, the temple was established around 150 years ago after a shepherd experienced a divine vision of the Goddess. The present structure has been renovated several times by local villagers.
history.location: Near Pipalkoti Village, Neelkanth Mahadev Road, Rishikesh, Uttarakhand.
history.architecture: The temple is built in traditional North Indian style on a hilltop. Devotees climb around 327–400 steps to reach the shrine, which offers magnificent views of the surrounding mountains and valleys.
history.importance: The temple is considered a Siddh Peeth where devotees pray for marriage, family happiness, protection, and fulfillment of wishes. It is especially popular among pilgrims visiting Neelkanth Mahadev Temple.

story: Welcome to the sacred Maa Bal Kumari Temple, nestled amidst the serene Himalayan hills near Neelkanth Mahadev. According to local belief, Maa Bal Kumari is the youthful form of Goddess Parvati, symbolizing purity, strength, and divine grace. It is believed that a local shepherd received divine blessings from the Goddess at this very spot, leading to the establishment of the temple. Today, devotees climb hundreds of steps through lush forests to seek the blessings of the Goddess and enjoy the peaceful spiritual atmosphere. The temple is also famous for its breathtaking mountain views and tranquil surroundings.
seasonal_note: October to April is the best time to visit. During Navratri, thousands of devotees visit the temple.

mangala_aarti.description: Morning prayers dedicated to Maa Bal Kumari.

bhog_aarti.description: Midday Bhog offered to the Goddess.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening Aarti with devotional bhajans.

shringar_aarti.description: Special adornment of Maa Bal Kumari.

best_visit_time: Early morning (6:00 AM–8:00 AM) or evening before sunset for pleasant weather and scenic views.
entry_fee: Entry is free for all visitors.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended.

name: Daksheshwar Mahadev Temple

history.overview: Daksheshwar Mahadev Temple is one of the most sacred Shiva temples in Haridwar. Located in Kankhal, it is deeply associated with the legendary story of King Daksha, Goddess Sati, and Lord Shiva, making it an important pilgrimage site for devotees.
history.built_by: The present temple was constructed in 1810 by Queen Dhankaur of Landhaura and has undergone several renovations while preserving its religious significance.
history.architecture: The temple is built in traditional North Indian Hindu style with beautifully decorated domes, a spacious courtyard, and shrines dedicated to Lord Shiva, Goddess Sati, and other deities. The temple complex also includes the sacred Yajna Kund associated with the Daksha Yajna.
history.importance: Daksheshwar Mahadev Temple marks the site where King Daksha performed the famous yajna that led to Goddess Sati's self-immolation and Lord Shiva's divine fury. It is one of the most significant Shiva temples in Uttarakhand and attracts thousands of devotees throughout the year.

story: Welcome to the sacred Daksheshwar Mahadev Temple, one of the holiest shrines dedicated to Lord Shiva. This temple stands on the legendary site where King Daksha organized a grand yajna but deliberately did not invite his son-in-law, Lord Shiva. Unable to bear the insult to her husband, Goddess Sati sacrificed herself in the sacred fire. Filled with grief and anger, Lord Shiva created Veerabhadra, who destroyed the yajna and beheaded King Daksha. Later, Shiva forgave Daksha and restored his life by replacing his head with that of a goat. This powerful legend symbolizes devotion, sacrifice, forgiveness, and the triumph of divine justice. Today, devotees visit this temple seeking Lord Shiva's blessings for peace, strength, and spiritual well-being.
seasonal_note: October to March offers pleasant weather for visiting. During Shravan month and Maha Shivaratri, the temple experiences a large influx of devotees.

mangala_aarti.description: Morning Mangala Aarti dedicated to Lord Shiva.

bhog_aarti.description: Midday Bhog offering to Lord Shiva.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening Aarti performed with devotional chants.

shringar_aarti.description: Special decoration and worship of the Shiva Lingam.

shayan_aarti.description: Final prayers before the temple closes.

best_visit_time: Early morning (5:30 AM–8:00 AM) or evening during Sandhya Aarti for a peaceful spiritual experience.
entry_fee: Entry is free for all visitors.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Footwear must be removed before entering the temple.

name: Mansa Devi Temple

history.overview: Mansa Devi Temple is one of the most revered Shakti temples in North India. Situated atop Bilwa Parvat in Haridwar, it is one of the Siddh Peethas where devotees come to seek the blessings of Goddess Mansa Devi, believed to fulfill sincere wishes.
history.built_by: The present temple was constructed between 1811 and 1815 by Maharaja Gopal Singh of Manimajra. It has since undergone several renovations while preserving its spiritual significance.
history.architecture: The temple features traditional North Indian Hindu architecture with beautifully decorated sanctums and panoramic views of Haridwar, the Ganga River, and the surrounding Shivalik Hills. It is accessible by ropeway as well as a trekking route.
history.importance: Mansa Devi Temple is one of the Panch Tirths of Haridwar and forms the famous Siddh Peeth Triangle along with Chandi Devi Temple and Maya Devi Temple. It attracts millions of devotees, especially during Navratri and the Kumbh Mela.

story: Welcome to the sacred Mansa Devi Temple, the divine abode of Goddess Mansa Devi, the wish-fulfilling form of Adi Shakti. Perched on the serene Bilwa Parvat overlooking the holy city of Haridwar, this temple has welcomed pilgrims for centuries. Devotees tie sacred threads on the temple premises while making heartfelt wishes and return to untie them once their wishes are fulfilled. The breathtaking views of the Ganga River, Har Ki Pauri, and the surrounding hills add to the temple's spiritual charm. Whether you reach by the scenic ropeway or by trekking through the peaceful hillside, a visit to Mansa Devi Temple offers a unique blend of devotion, faith, and natural beauty.
seasonal_note: October to April is considered the best time to visit due to pleasant weather. During Navratri and Kumbh Mela, the temple experiences very large crowds.

mangala_aarti.description: Morning Mangala Aarti dedicated to Goddess Mansa Devi.

bhog_aarti.description: Midday Bhog offering to the Goddess.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening Aarti performed with devotional hymns.

shringar_aarti.description: Special adornment and worship of Goddess Mansa Devi.

shayan_aarti.description: Final prayers before the temple closes for the day.

best_visit_time: Early morning (5:30 AM–8:00 AM) or evening before sunset to enjoy peaceful darshan and panoramic views of Haridwar.
entry_fee: Temple entry is free. Ropeway tickets are chargeable.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Visitors are advised to remove footwear before entering the temple.

name: Omkareshwar Temple

history.overview: Omkareshwar Temple is one of the twelve sacred Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva. It is situated on the holy Mandhata Island in the Narmada River, whose shape resembles the sacred Hindu symbol 'Om'.
history.built_by: The temple has ancient origins and has been renovated by several Hindu dynasties including the Paramaras, Marathas, and Holkars over different periods.
history.location: Mandhata Island, Omkareshwar, Khandwa District, Madhya Pradesh.
history.architecture: Built in the traditional Nagara style, the temple features intricately carved stone pillars, a multi-storied structure, and beautiful sculptures overlooking the Narmada River.
history.importance: It is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas and holds immense spiritual significance. Devotees believe that worshipping here fulfills wishes and grants liberation from sins.

story: Welcome to the sacred Omkareshwar Temple, one of the twelve divine Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva. According to ancient legends, the Devas once prayed to Lord Shiva here to protect them from powerful demons. Pleased by their devotion, Shiva manifested Himself as the sacred Jyotirlinga of Omkareshwar. Another legend states that King Mandhata of the Ikshvaku dynasty performed intense penance here, making this island a holy pilgrimage site. The island itself resembles the sacred symbol 'Om', representing the eternal sound of the universe. Surrounded by the serene waters of the Narmada River, Omkareshwar offers devotees a peaceful atmosphere for prayer, meditation, and spiritual awakening. May Lord Omkareshwar bless you with strength, wisdom, prosperity, and inner peace.
seasonal_note: October to March is the best time to visit due to pleasant weather. During Shravan month and Maha Shivaratri, the temple witnesses a huge influx of devotees.
break_time: Darshan may be temporarily paused during special rituals and major festivals.

mangala_aarti.description: Subah ki pehli Mangala Aarti mein Bhagwan Shiva ka divya darshan hota hai.

bhog_aarti.description: Dopahar mein Bhagwan ko bhog arpit kiya jata hai.

sandhya_aarti.description: Sandhya ke samay Narmada kinare bhavya aarti hoti hai.

shringar_aarti.description: Shivling ka sundar shringar kiya jata hai.

shayan_aarti.description: Raat ki antim aarti ke baad Bhagwan Shiva ko shayan karaya jata hai.

best_visit_time: Early morning during Mangala Aarti (5:00 AM–7:00 AM) or evening during Sandhya Aarti (7:00 PM).
entry_fee: General entry is free. VIP/Special Darshan passes may be available during peak seasons.
dress_code: Traditional Indian attire is preferred. Visitors should wear modest clothing covering shoulders and knees.

name: Maa Bamleshwari Temple

history.overview: Maa Bamleshwari Temple is one of the most revered Shakti Peethas of Chhattisgarh. Situated atop a hill about 1,600 feet high in Dongargarh, the temple attracts lakhs of devotees every year.
history.built_by: According to local tradition, the temple was established over 2,200 years ago by King Veersen after receiving the blessings of Goddess Bamleshwari. The temple has undergone several renovations over the centuries.
history.architecture: The temple is built in the traditional Nagara style and is located on a hill reached by nearly 1,000 steps or a ropeway. The temple offers panoramic views of the surrounding hills and lakes.
history.importance: Dedicated to Goddess Bamleshwari, a form of Maa Durga, it is one of the most important pilgrimage centers in Central India. The temple witnesses massive gatherings during both Chaitra and Sharadiya Navratri.

story: Welcome to the sacred Maa Bamleshwari Temple, one of the holiest Shakti shrines in Chhattisgarh. According to legend, King Veersen had no children and prayed with deep devotion to Goddess Bamleshwari. Pleased with his prayers, the Goddess blessed him with a son. In gratitude, the king built this magnificent temple atop the hill. Another popular legend says that King Vikramaditya once came here in despair, but Goddess Bamleshwari appeared before him and restored his faith. Today, devotees climb nearly one thousand steps or use the ropeway to seek the blessings of the Divine Mother. Surrounded by beautiful hills and serene landscapes, the temple symbolizes devotion, courage, and divine grace. May Maa Bamleshwari bless you with happiness, prosperity, and protection.
seasonal_note: October to March offers pleasant weather. During both Navratri festivals, lakhs of devotees visit the temple and a grand fair is organized.
break_time: No fixed break. Darshan may pause briefly during special rituals.

mangala_aarti.description: Subah Maa Bamleshwari ki Mangala Aarti hoti hai.

bhog_aarti.description: Dopahar mein Maa ko bhog arpit kiya jata hai.

sandhya_aarti.description: Sham ki bhavya Sandhya Aarti mein hazaron bhakt shamil hote hain.

shringar_aarti.description: Maa ka divya shringar kiya jata hai.

shayan_aarti.description: Raat ki antim aarti ke baad mandir band kiya jata hai.

best_visit_time: Early morning (5:30 AM–8:00 AM) or evening during Sandhya Aarti. Navratri is the most vibrant time to visit.
entry_fee: General entry is free. Ropeway charges are applicable.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended.

name: Amarnath Cave Temple

history.overview: Amarnath Cave Temple is one of the holiest shrines dedicated to Lord Shiva. Situated at an altitude of about 3,888 meters in the Himalayas, the temple is famous for the naturally formed ice Shivling, which is considered a divine manifestation of Lord Shiva.
history.built_by: The cave is a natural formation and has been revered by devotees for centuries. Historical records mention its rediscovery by the Muslim shepherd Buta Malik several hundred years ago, although references to the shrine exist in ancient Hindu scriptures.
history.location: Near Pahalgam, Anantnag District, Jammu and Kashmir.
history.architecture: The shrine is a natural limestone cave surrounded by snow-covered Himalayan peaks. The naturally formed ice Shivling is created every year by the freezing of water droplets inside the cave.
history.importance: Amarnath Cave Temple is one of the most sacred pilgrimage sites for Hindus and forms an important part of the annual Amarnath Yatra. Thousands of devotees undertake the challenging pilgrimage every year to seek the blessings of Lord Shiva.

story: Welcome to the sacred Amarnath Cave Temple. According to Hindu mythology, Lord Shiva chose this remote Himalayan cave to reveal the secret of immortality, known as the Amar Katha, to Goddess Parvati. Before entering the cave, Lord Shiva is believed to have left behind Nandi, the Moon, the snakes, Lord Ganesha, and the five elements to ensure complete secrecy. During this divine discourse, two pigeon eggs lying inside the cave accidentally heard the sacred knowledge and became immortal. Even today, many pilgrims believe that sightings of a pair of pigeons near the cave are a symbol of this divine legend. The naturally formed ice Shivling is worshipped as a sacred manifestation of Lord Shiva and attracts millions of devotees every year.
opening_time: Open only during the annual Amarnath Yatra (typically June–August)
closing_time: Closed for the rest of the year due to heavy snowfall
seasonal_note: The temple remains accessible only during the official Amarnath Yatra season. The cave remains closed during winter because of extreme weather conditions and heavy snowfall.

morning_darshan.description: Pilgrims begin darshan of the naturally formed ice Shivling.

rudrabhishek.description: Special Rudrabhishek and worship are performed during the pilgrimage season.

afternoon_pooja.description: Devotional prayers and offerings continue throughout the day.

evening_aarti.description: Evening Aarti is performed before the shrine closes for the day.

best_visit_time: June to August during the official Amarnath Yatra.
entry_fee: Entry is free. Pilgrims must complete mandatory registration and obtain a Yatra Permit issued by the Shri Amarnath Shrine Board.
dress_code: Warm woollen clothing, trekking shoes, rain protection, and modest attire are strongly recommended due to the cold Himalayan climate.

restaurants.name: Langar Sewa Camps (free community kitchens along route)

name: Lotus Temple

history.overview: The Lotus Temple is a Baháʼí House of Worship in New Delhi, renowned worldwide for its unique lotus-shaped architecture. It is open to people of all religions, faiths, and backgrounds, promoting unity, peace, and harmony.
history.built_by: The temple was designed by the Iranian architect Fariborz Sahba and completed in 1986. It was built by the Baháʼí community of India.
history.architecture: The temple is inspired by the shape of a blooming lotus flower and consists of 27 free-standing white marble petals arranged in clusters. It has won numerous international architectural awards.
history.importance: The Lotus Temple is one of the most visited religious monuments in the world and serves as a symbol of peace, unity, and the oneness of humanity.

story: Welcome to the Lotus Temple. Inspired by the purity and beauty of the lotus flower, this magnificent Baháʼí House of Worship welcomes people from every religion and culture. Unlike traditional temples, the Lotus Temple has no idols, rituals, or sermons. Visitors gather here in silence for prayer, meditation, and spiritual reflection. Since its inauguration in 1986, it has become one of India's most iconic landmarks and a global symbol of unity, peace, and harmony. Surrounded by beautiful gardens and tranquil pools, the Lotus Temple offers a serene environment where every visitor can experience inner peace and spiritual calm.

infobox.Dedicated To: Unity of Humanity and Worship of One God

seasonal_note: The Lotus Temple remains open throughout the year. It is generally closed on Mondays. The best time to visit is during the cooler months from October to March.

morning_prayer.description: Silent prayer and meditation session open to visitors of all faiths.

midday_prayer.description: Reading of sacred scriptures from different religions in a peaceful atmosphere.

evening_prayer.description: Quiet meditation and devotional readings before closing.

best_visit_time: October to March. Early morning (9:00 AM–11:00 AM) or late afternoon (3:30 PM–5:00 PM) for a peaceful visit.
entry_fee: Entry is completely free for all visitors.
dress_code: Visitors are expected to dress modestly and maintain silence inside the prayer hall.

name: Badrinath Temple

history.overview: Badrinath Temple is one of the holiest Hindu temples dedicated to Lord Vishnu in his form as Badrinarayan. It is one of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites and also one of the 108 Divya Desams revered by Vaishnavites.
history.built_by: The temple is believed to have been originally established by Adi Shankaracharya in the 8th century CE. The present structure has undergone several renovations by the Garhwal kings and other devotees over the centuries.
history.architecture: Built in the traditional North Indian Himalayan style, the temple features a colorful facade, a gold-gilded roof, a conical tower (shikhara), and a sanctum housing the black stone idol of Lord Badrinarayan.
history.importance: Badrinath Temple is one of the four Char Dham pilgrimage sites and one of the most sacred temples dedicated to Lord Vishnu. Every year, millions of devotees visit the temple seeking divine blessings.

story: Welcome to the sacred Badrinath Temple. According to Hindu tradition, Lord Vishnu performed deep meditation at this holy place while Goddess Lakshmi took the form of the Badri tree to protect Him from harsh weather. Pleased with her devotion, Lord Vishnu declared that the place would forever be known as Badrinath. The temple is also closely associated with Adi Shankaracharya, who rediscovered the idol of Lord Badrinarayan in the Alaknanda River and re-established the temple as a major pilgrimage center. Surrounded by the majestic Himalayas and situated on the banks of the Alaknanda River, Badrinath offers devotees an atmosphere of profound peace and spiritual enlightenment. May Lord Badrinarayan bless you with health, wisdom, prosperity, and divine grace.

infobox.Established: Ancient (Revived in the 8th century CE by Adi Shankaracharya)

seasonal_note: The temple remains open only during the summer pilgrimage season, generally from late April or early May until October or November. It remains closed during winter due to heavy snowfall.

maha_abhishek.description: The temple opens with the sacred Maha Abhishek ritual performed for Lord Badrinarayan.

morning_pooja.description: Morning prayers, Vedic chanting, and offerings are performed.

bal_bhog.description: Food offerings are presented to Lord Badrinarayan.

geet_govind.description: Evening devotional hymns from the Geet Govind are sung before the deity.

shayan_aarti.description: The final Shayan Aarti is performed before the temple closes for the night.

best_visit_time: May to June and September to October. Early morning darshan (5:00 AM–8:00 AM) offers the most peaceful experience.
entry_fee: General temple entry is free. Charges may apply for special pujas and VIP darshan.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Warm clothing is advised due to the cold Himalayan climate.

name: Banke Bihari Temple

history.overview: Banke Bihari Temple is one of the most famous temples dedicated to Lord Krishna and is a major pilgrimage site in Vrindavan.
history.built_by: The present temple was built in the 19th century by the disciples and descendants of the saint Swami Haridas.
history.architecture: Built in traditional Rajasthani style with intricate stone carvings and spacious courtyards.
history.importance: The temple is renowned for the unique tradition of opening and closing the curtain during darshan and is closely associated with the divine pastimes of Lord Krishna.

story: Welcome to the sacred Banke Bihari Temple. According to tradition, the great saint Swami Haridas performed deep meditation in Nidhivan, where Lord Radha and Lord Krishna appeared before him in their divine form. At the saint's request, They merged into a single beautiful idol known today as Banke Bihari Ji. The name 'Banke' means gracefully bent in three places, while 'Bihari' means the supreme enjoyer. Even today, the temple follows the unique tradition of repeatedly opening and closing the curtain during darshan, as devotees believe that the loving gaze of Banke Bihari Ji is so captivating that it can completely absorb the heart of every devotee. May Lord Krishna bless you with joy, love, and peace.

mangala_aarti.description: Mangala Aarti is performed only once a year on the occasion of Janmashtami.

shringar_aarti.description: The deity is beautifully adorned with ornaments and flowers.

rajbhog_aarti.description: The main midday offering of food is presented to the Lord.

utthapan_aarti.description: Evening darshan resumes after the afternoon break.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening prayers are offered with devotional songs.

shayan_aarti.description: The final ritual before the temple closes for the day.

best_visit_time: Early morning (8:00 AM–10:00 AM) or evening (5:30 PM–8:00 PM).
entry_fee: Free
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended.

name: Krishna Janmabhoomi Temple

history.overview: Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi Temple is one of the holiest pilgrimage sites in India and is believed to be the birthplace of Lord Krishna.
history.built_by: The present temple complex was reconstructed in the 20th century after several historical destructions and restorations over the centuries.
history.architecture: The temple complex features the sacred Garbha Griha, believed to be the birthplace of Lord Krishna, along with beautifully designed shrines and halls.
history.importance: It is one of the most important pilgrimage destinations for devotees of Lord Krishna and attracts millions of visitors every year, especially during Janmashtami.

story: Welcome to the sacred Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi Temple. This holy place is believed to be the exact birthplace of Lord Krishna. According to Hindu tradition, Lord Krishna was born here over five thousand years ago in a prison cell where His parents, Vasudeva and Devaki, were imprisoned by the cruel King Kansa. Soon after His birth, baby Krishna was safely carried across the Yamuna River to Gokul, where He was lovingly raised by Nanda and Yashoda. Over the centuries, this sacred site has been rebuilt many times and continues to stand as a symbol of faith, devotion, and hope. Today, millions of devotees visit this temple to seek the blessings of Lord Krishna. May Lord Krishna fill your life with happiness, wisdom, and peace.

infobox.Established: Ancient (Present complex rebuilt in the 20th century)

mangala_aarti.description: Morning prayers begin with Mangala Aarti.

shringar_aarti.description: The deity is beautifully adorned with flowers and ornaments.

rajbhog_aarti.description: The main midday offering is presented to Lord Krishna.

utthapan_aarti.description: Afternoon darshan resumes after the temple reopens.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening prayers are offered with devotional hymns.

shayan_aarti.description: The final ritual before the temple closes for the day.

best_visit_time: Early morning (5:00 AM–8:00 AM) or during Janmashtami celebrations.
entry_fee: Free
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended.

name: Kedarnath Temple

history.overview: Kedarnath Temple is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva and one of the holiest temples in India. It is also an important shrine of the Char Dham and Panch Kedar pilgrimages.
history.built_by: The temple is believed to have been originally built by the Pandavas and later restored by Adi Shankaracharya in the 8th century CE.
history.architecture: Built in traditional Himalayan style using massive grey stone slabs without modern cement, the temple has stood strong for centuries despite extreme weather conditions.
history.importance: Situated at an altitude of about 3,583 meters, Kedarnath is one of the most sacred pilgrimage destinations dedicated to Lord Shiva and attracts millions of devotees every year.

story: Welcome to the sacred Kedarnath Temple, one of the twelve Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva. According to Hindu tradition, after the Mahabharata war, the Pandavas came here seeking Lord Shiva's forgiveness for the sins of war. Lord Shiva took the form of a bull to avoid them, but when the Pandavas recognized Him, He disappeared into the earth, leaving His hump at Kedarnath. This sacred form is worshipped today as the Jyotirlinga, while the remaining parts appeared at the other Panch Kedar temples. Surrounded by the majestic Himalayas and the Mandakini River, Kedarnath is a symbol of faith, devotion, and resilience. Despite the devastating floods of 2013, the temple remained standing, inspiring millions of devotees around the world. May Lord Kedarnath bless you with strength, peace, and divine grace.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open from April/May to November and stays closed during winter due to heavy snowfall.

pratah_aarti.description: Morning prayers begin with the Pratah Aarti.

abhishek_aarti.description: The sacred Shivling is bathed and worshipped with traditional rituals.

evening_aarti.description: The evening Aarti fills the temple with devotional chants and spiritual energy.

name: Somnath Temple

history.overview: Somnath Temple is the first of the twelve Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva and is one of the most sacred pilgrimage sites in India.
history.built_by: According to tradition, the original temple was built by Chandra (the Moon God). The present temple was reconstructed in the Chalukya style after India's independence under the initiative of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel and was inaugurated in 1951.
history.architecture: Built in the traditional Chalukya style, the temple features a magnificent sandstone structure with a 155-foot-tall Shikhara and beautifully carved pillars.
history.importance: Somnath is regarded as the first Jyotirlinga of Lord Shiva and stands as a symbol of faith, resilience, and India's rich spiritual heritage.

story: Welcome to the sacred Somnath Temple, the first of the twelve Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva. According to ancient tradition, Chandra, the Moon God, worshipped Lord Shiva here to seek relief from a curse. Pleased with his devotion, Lord Shiva blessed him and became known as Somnath, meaning 'Lord of the Moon.' Throughout history, this temple faced repeated invasions and was rebuilt many times, becoming a lasting symbol of courage and unwavering faith. Today, standing proudly on the shores of the Arabian Sea, Somnath Temple continues to inspire millions of devotees from around the world. As you offer your prayers, may Lord Shiva bless you with strength, peace, and prosperity.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year.

pratah_aarti.description: Morning prayers are offered to Lord Shiva.

madhyan_aarti.description: Midday worship and offerings are presented to the Jyotirlinga.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening Aarti is performed, followed by the famous Sound and Light Show.

best_visit_time: Early morning for peaceful darshan or in the evening to attend the Aarti and the Sound and Light Show.
entry_fee: General temple entry is free. Separate charges apply for the Sound and Light Show.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Leather items are not permitted inside the temple premises.

name: Dwarkadhish Temple

history.overview: Dwarkadhish Temple, also known as Jagat Mandir, is one of the most sacred temples dedicated to Lord Krishna and is one of the four Char Dham pilgrimage sites.
history.built_by: According to tradition, the original temple was built by Vajranabha, the great-grandson of Lord Krishna. The present temple was constructed between the 15th and 16th centuries.
history.architecture: Built in the traditional Chalukya style, the five-story temple is supported by 72 intricately carved pillars and crowned by a magnificent 78-meter-high Shikhara.
history.importance: The temple is dedicated to Lord Krishna as the King of Dwarka and is one of the holiest pilgrimage destinations in Hinduism.

story: Welcome to the sacred Dwarkadhish Temple, the divine kingdom of Lord Krishna. According to Hindu tradition, after leaving Mathura, Lord Krishna established the magnificent city of Dwarka and ruled here as its beloved king. It is believed that the original temple was built by His great-grandson Vajranabha in memory of Lord Krishna. The present temple, with its towering Shikhara and beautifully carved pillars, has inspired devotees for centuries. One of its unique traditions is the changing of the sacred temple flag several times each day, symbolizing continuous devotion to the Lord. As you stand before Dwarkadhish, may Lord Krishna bless you with wisdom, happiness, and everlasting peace.

infobox.Established: Ancient (Present structure built during the 15th–16th centuries)

seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year.

name: Tirupati Balaji Temple

history.overview: Tirumala Venkateswara Temple, popularly known as Tirupati Balaji Temple, is one of the most sacred and visited Hindu temples in the world, dedicated to Lord Venkateswara, an incarnation of Lord Vishnu.
history.built_by: The temple has a history spanning over a thousand years and received major patronage from the Pallava, Chola, and Vijayanagara dynasties, especially Emperor Krishnadevaraya.
history.architecture: Built in the traditional Dravidian style, the temple features magnificent gopurams, the gold-plated Ananda Nilayam Vimana, and a richly adorned idol of Lord Venkateswara.
history.importance: One of the 108 Divya Desams, the temple is believed to be the earthly abode of Lord Vishnu during the Kali Yuga and welcomes millions of devotees every year.

story: Welcome to the sacred Tirupati Balaji Temple, the divine abode of Lord Venkateswara. According to Hindu tradition, Lord Vishnu manifested here during the Kali Yuga to protect humanity and bless His devotees with peace, prosperity, and salvation. Situated atop the seven sacred Tirumala Hills, this temple has inspired millions of pilgrims for centuries with its spiritual atmosphere and timeless traditions. One of the temple's unique customs is the offering of hair by devotees as a symbol of humility and devotion. The famous Tirupati Laddu Prasadam, offered after darshan, is cherished by devotees from around the world. As you seek the blessings of Lord Venkateswara, may your life be filled with happiness, success, and divine grace.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year and experiences heavy crowds during festivals and weekends.

suprabhatam.description: The day begins with Suprabhatam, awakening Lord Venkateswara with sacred hymns.

tomala_seva.description: The deity is adorned with beautiful flower garlands.

dolotsavam.description: Evening seva performed with devotional music and rituals.

best_visit_time: Early morning for peaceful darshan. Advance booking is recommended for Special Entry Darshan.

dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Certain sevas may require traditional dress such as dhoti, saree, or salwar kameez.

name: Meenakshi Amman Temple

history.overview: Meenakshi Amman Temple is one of the most magnificent Hindu temples in India, dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi, an incarnation of Goddess Parvati, and Lord Sundareswarar (Shiva).
history.built_by: The present temple was largely rebuilt and expanded during the 16th and 17th centuries by the Nayak rulers, especially under Ariyanatha Mudaliar.
history.architecture: Built in the traditional Dravidian style, the temple is famous for its 14 towering gopurams, thousands of colorful sculptures, the Hall of Thousand Pillars, and the Golden Lotus Tank.
history.importance: The temple is one of the most important pilgrimage sites in South India and is celebrated as a symbol of devotion, art, and Tamil culture.

story: Welcome to the magnificent Meenakshi Amman Temple. According to Hindu tradition, Goddess Meenakshi, the divine queen of Madurai and an incarnation of Goddess Parvati, was born to rule the Pandya Kingdom. She later married Lord Sundareswarar, a form of Lord Shiva, in a grand celestial wedding celebrated as the union of Shakti and Shiva. Every year, this sacred event is reenacted during the famous Meenakshi Tirukalyanam festival. The temple is renowned for its towering gopurams, vibrant sculptures, and remarkable Dravidian architecture that reflect centuries of artistic excellence. As you seek the blessings of Goddess Meenakshi and Lord Sundareswarar, may your life be filled with happiness, prosperity, and divine grace.

infobox.Established: Ancient (Present structure rebuilt during the 16th–17th centuries)

seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year and receives the largest number of visitors during the Chithirai Festival.

name: Brihadeeswarar Temple

history.overview: Brihadeeswarar Temple, also known as the Big Temple, is one of India's greatest architectural masterpieces dedicated to Lord Shiva and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
history.built_by: The temple was built in 1010 CE by Emperor Rajaraja Chola I of the Chola dynasty.
history.architecture: Constructed entirely from granite in the Dravidian style, the temple is famous for its 66-meter-tall Vimana, massive monolithic Nandi, intricate sculptures, and Chola-era frescoes.
history.importance: It is one of the finest examples of Chola architecture and remains an active place of worship as well as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

story: Welcome to the magnificent Brihadeeswarar Temple, one of the greatest architectural wonders of India. Built over one thousand years ago by the great Chola emperor Rajaraja Chola I, this temple stands as a timeless symbol of devotion, engineering, and artistic excellence. Dedicated to Lord Shiva, the temple is renowned for its towering granite Vimana, magnificent Nandi statue carved from a single stone, and beautifully preserved sculptures and inscriptions. Even after a millennium, Brihadeeswarar Temple continues to inspire devotees and visitors with its grandeur and spiritual atmosphere. As you offer your prayers here, may Lord Shiva bless you with wisdom, strength, and inner peace.

infobox.UNESCO Status: Great Living Chola Temples World Heritage Site

seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year.

name: Jagannath Temple

history.overview: Jagannath Temple is one of the most sacred Hindu temples dedicated to Lord Jagannath, a form of Lord Vishnu (Krishna), and is one of the four Char Dham pilgrimage sites.
history.built_by: The present temple was built in the 12th century CE by King Anantavarman Chodaganga Deva of the Eastern Ganga dynasty.
history.architecture: Built in the traditional Kalinga style, the temple features a majestic 65-meter-high Shikhara, intricately carved structures, and one of the world's largest temple kitchens.
history.importance: The temple is famous for the annual Rath Yatra, the sacred Mahaprasad, and the unique Nabakalebara ceremony, during which the wooden idols are ceremonially replaced.

story: Welcome to the sacred Jagannath Temple, one of the holiest pilgrimage destinations in India. Lord Jagannath, along with His elder brother Lord Balabhadra and sister Goddess Subhadra, is worshipped here with great devotion. According to tradition, the divine idols are carved from sacred neem wood and are ceremonially renewed during the rare Nabakalebara festival. The temple is world-famous for the magnificent Rath Yatra, when the three deities travel through the streets of Puri in beautifully decorated wooden chariots, attracting millions of devotees from across the world. The temple is also renowned for its sacred Mahaprasad, prepared daily in one of the world's largest temple kitchens. May Lord Jagannath bless you with peace, happiness, and divine grace.

infobox.Dedicated To: Lord Jagannath, Lord Balabhadra, and Goddess Subhadra

seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. The Rath Yatra festival is the most popular time to visit.

name: Vaishno Devi Temple

history.overview: Vaishno Devi Temple is one of the holiest Hindu pilgrimage sites dedicated to Goddess Vaishno Devi, who is revered as a manifestation of Maha Kali, Maha Lakshmi, and Maha Saraswati.
history.built_by: The shrine is an ancient natural cave with no known founder. It has been revered by devotees for centuries and is currently managed by the Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Shrine Board.
history.location: Trikuta Hills near Katra, Jammu and Kashmir.
history.architecture: The shrine is a sacred natural cave housing three holy rock formations, known as the Pindis, which represent the three divine forms of the Goddess.
history.importance: One of India's most visited pilgrimage destinations, the temple is believed to fulfill the sincere prayers of devotees who undertake the sacred journey with devotion.

story: Welcome to the sacred shrine of Mata Vaishno Devi. According to Hindu tradition, Goddess Vaishno Devi performed deep meditation in this holy cave to protect righteousness and guide humanity. When the demon Bhairon Nath pursued Her, the Goddess revealed Her divine form and defeated him. Before attaining liberation, Bhairon Nath sought forgiveness, and the Goddess blessed him by declaring that every pilgrimage to Vaishno Devi would be considered complete only after visiting the Bhairon Temple. Inside the holy cave, devotees worship the three sacred Pindis, representing Maha Kali, Maha Lakshmi, and Maha Saraswati. As you undertake this sacred journey through the Trikuta Hills, may Mata Rani bless you with strength, peace, and prosperity.

infobox.Managed By: Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Shrine Board

seasonal_note: The shrine remains open throughout the year. Heavy crowds are expected during both Navratri festivals.

name: Shrinathji Temple

history.overview: Shrinathji Temple is one of the most revered Hindu temples dedicated to Lord Shrinathji, a child form of Lord Krishna lifting the Govardhan Hill and the principal shrine of the Pushtimarg tradition.
history.built_by: The sacred idol was moved from Govardhan near Mathura to Nathdwara in 1672 CE by followers of the Pushtimarg tradition under the guidance of Goswami descendants of Vallabhacharya.
history.architecture: Unlike a traditional temple, the shrine is designed as a royal Haveli (mansion), where Lord Shrinathji is served as the King of the household through daily rituals.
history.importance: The temple is the spiritual center of the Pushtimarg tradition and is famous for its Ashtayam Seva, Pichwai paintings, and devotion to Lord Krishna.

story: Welcome to the sacred Shrinathji Temple of Nathdwara. According to tradition, the divine idol of Lord Shrinathji originally resided at Govardhan near Mathura. During the Mughal period, devotees carried the idol to a safer place. As the bullock cart reached Nathdwara, its wheels became firmly stuck and would not move despite every effort. This was understood as Lord Shrinathji's wish to reside here forever. Since then, Nathdwara has become one of the holiest centers of Krishna devotion. The temple follows the unique Pushtimarg tradition, where the Lord is lovingly served as a living child through eight daily darshans, beautiful garments, devotional music, and delicious offerings. May Lord Shrinathji bless you with happiness, devotion, and prosperity.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. Festival days witness very large crowds.
break_time: Darshan remains closed between different Sevas throughout the day.

name: Ramanathaswamy Temple

history.overview: Ramanathaswamy Temple is one of the holiest temples dedicated to Lord Shiva and is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas as well as one of the four Char Dham pilgrimage sites.
history.built_by: According to tradition, the original shrine was established by Lord Rama. The present temple was expanded over several centuries by the Pandya kings, the Sethupathi rulers, and other South Indian dynasties.
history.architecture: Built in the magnificent Dravidian style, the temple is famous for its towering gopurams, intricate stone carvings, and the world's longest temple corridor with more than 1,200 beautifully carved pillars.
history.importance: The temple is closely associated with the Ramayana, where Lord Rama worshipped Lord Shiva before returning to Ayodhya after defeating Ravana.

story: Welcome to the sacred Ramanathaswamy Temple, one of the holiest shrines dedicated to Lord Shiva. According to the Ramayana, after defeating Ravana in Lanka, Lord Rama wished to seek forgiveness for the sin of killing a great scholar and devotee of Lord Shiva. He installed a Shiva Linga at this sacred place and worshipped Lord Shiva with deep devotion. It is believed that Hanuman was sent to bring a Linga from the Himalayas, but when he was delayed, Goddess Sita created a sand Linga, which became the revered Ramanathaswamy Jyotirlinga. The temple is also famous for its 22 sacred wells, where pilgrims perform ritual baths before offering prayers. As you visit this holy temple, may Lord Shiva and Lord Rama bless you with peace, devotion, and spiritual strength.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. October to March offers pleasant weather for pilgrims.

name: Kamakhya Temple

history.overview: Kamakhya Temple is one of the most sacred temples dedicated to Goddess Kamakhya, an incarnation of Goddess Shakti. It is one of the 51 Shakti Peethas and among the most important centers of Tantric worship in India.
history.built_by: The original temple is believed to date back to ancient times. The present structure was rebuilt in the 16th century by King Naranarayana of the Koch dynasty after the earlier temple was destroyed.
history.architecture: The temple is built in the unique Nilachal style, featuring a beehive-shaped dome, beautifully carved stone walls, and intricate sculptures of Hindu deities.
history.importance: According to Hindu tradition, this is the sacred place where Goddess Sati's womb and yoni fell, making it one of the holiest Shakti Peethas and a powerful symbol of feminine divine energy.

story: Welcome to the sacred Kamakhya Temple, one of the most powerful shrines of Goddess Shakti. Long ago, when Goddess Sati sacrificed herself during King Daksha's great yajna, Lord Shiva carried her body across the universe in deep sorrow. To restore balance, Lord Vishnu used His Sudarshan Chakra to divide Sati's body into many sacred parts. It is believed that Her divine womb fell on this very hill, making Kamakhya one of the holiest Shakti Peethas. Unlike most temples, there is no idol here. Instead, devotees worship a naturally formed sacred stone, continuously nourished by an underground spring. Every year, during the Ambubachi Mela, the temple celebrates the divine power of creation and motherhood. As you stand in this sacred place, may Goddess Kamakhya bless you with strength, wisdom, prosperity, and inner peace.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. June is extremely crowded during the Ambubachi Mela.

entry_fee: General entry is free. Special darshan tickets are available.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Footwear must be removed before entering the temple.

name: Kanaka Durga Temple

history.overview: Kanaka Durga Temple is one of the most revered temples dedicated to Goddess Durga and is among the most important Shakti shrines in South India.
history.built_by: The temple is believed to have ancient origins. The present structure has been renovated and expanded by several South Indian dynasties and is now managed by the Sri Durga Malleswara Swamy Varla Devasthanam.
history.architecture: Built in the traditional Dravidian style, the temple stands atop Indrakeeladri Hill overlooking the Krishna River, with beautifully carved gopurams and sacred shrines.
history.importance: The Goddess is worshipped as the self-manifested (Swayambhu) Kanaka Durga. The temple is especially famous for its grand Dasara Navaratri celebrations, attracting millions of devotees every year.

story: Welcome to the sacred Kanaka Durga Temple, where faith and courage come together. Long ago, a devoted sage named Indrakeela performed intense penance, praying for the Divine Mother to bless the world. Pleased with his unwavering devotion, Goddess Durga appeared before him and chose to reside forever on the hill that came to be known as Indrakeeladri. Around the same time, the powerful demon Mahishasura spread fear across the heavens and the earth. To restore peace, the Goddess took the fierce form of Mahishasura Mardini and defeated him in a mighty battle, protecting all creation from evil. Since then, devotees have believed that Goddess Kanaka Durga grants courage, prosperity, and victory over every obstacle. As you stand before the Divine Mother today, may she fill your life with strength, wisdom, happiness, and endless blessings.

infobox.Managed By: Sri Durga Malleswara Swamy Varla Devasthanam

seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. During Dasara Navaratri, millions of devotees visit the shrine, so expect large crowds.

name: Siddhivinayak Temple

history.overview: Shree Siddhivinayak Temple is one of the most famous temples dedicated to Lord Ganesha and is among the most visited pilgrimage sites in India.
history.built_by: The temple was originally built on 19 November 1801 by Laxman Vithu Patil and Deubai Patil, a childless devotee who prayed that Lord Ganesha would bless all those seeking children and prosperity.
history.architecture: The temple combines traditional Hindu architecture with a magnificent gold-plated sanctum and beautifully carved domes. The idol of Lord Ganesha is carved from a single black stone and is unique because its trunk curves to the right.
history.importance: Lord Siddhivinayak is believed to remove obstacles and fulfill sincere wishes. Every Tuesday, millions of devotees visit the temple to seek the blessings of Ganpati Bappa.

story: Welcome to the sacred Siddhivinayak Temple, where millions of devotees begin their journey with the blessings of Lord Ganesha. Have you ever wondered why this temple is known as the temple where wishes come true? More than two hundred years ago, a humble woman named Deubai Patil, who had no children of her own, decided to build this temple with a selfless prayer. Instead of asking for a child, she prayed that every devotee who came here with faith would have their heartfelt wishes fulfilled. It is believed that Lord Ganesha accepted her prayer, and since then countless devotees have experienced His divine blessings. The unique idol, with its right-curved trunk, symbolizes immense spiritual power and wisdom. As you stand before Siddhivinayak, offer your prayers with a pure heart, and may Ganpati Bappa remove every obstacle from your path and fill your life with happiness, success, and prosperity.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. Tuesdays and Ganesh Chaturthi witness exceptionally large crowds.

dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Footwear must be removed before entering the temple.

name: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir

history.overview: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir is one of the holiest temples in Hinduism, believed to be built at the birthplace of Lord Shri Ram. The temple stands as a symbol of faith, devotion, and India's rich cultural heritage.
history.built_by: Constructed under the supervision of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. The temple was designed by the Sompura family of temple architects.
history.architecture: Built in the traditional Nagara style using pink sandstone from Rajasthan. The temple features beautifully carved pillars, grand mandapas, ornate sculptures, and a magnificent sanctum housing Ram Lalla.
history.importance: The temple marks the sacred birthplace of Lord Shri Ram and is one of the most important pilgrimage destinations for Hindus worldwide.

story: Welcome to Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir. According to the Ramayana and Hindu tradition, this sacred site is the birthplace of Lord Shri Ram, the seventh incarnation of Lord Vishnu. For centuries, devotees have revered Ayodhya as one of Hinduism's seven sacred cities. The grand temple represents faith, righteousness, and devotion, attracting millions of pilgrims who come to seek the blessings of Ram Lalla and experience the spiritual atmosphere of Ayodhya.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. October to March offers pleasant weather, while Ram Navami and Deepotsav witness the largest number of devotees.
break_time: Generally 01:00 PM – 03:00 PM (Darshan may pause briefly depending on temple schedule)

mangala_aarti.description: The day begins with Mangala Aarti and the first darshan of Ram Lalla.

shringar_aarti.description: The deity is adorned with beautiful ornaments and garments.

rajbhog_aarti.description: Midday offering of Rajbhog and special prayers.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening Aarti with devotional hymns, lamps, and bells.

best_visit_time: October to March. Early morning (6:30 AM–9:00 AM) or evening (5:00 PM–8:00 PM) offers a pleasant and peaceful darshan experience.
entry_fee: Entry is free for all devotees.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Visitors are advised to wear clothing that covers shoulders and knees as a mark of respect.

name: Trimbakeshwar Temple

history.overview: Trimbakeshwar Temple is one of the twelve sacred Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva and is renowned for its unique three-faced Jyotirlinga representing Brahma, Vishnu, and Mahesh.
history.built_by: The present temple was rebuilt in the 18th century by Peshwa Balaji Baji Rao (Nanasaheb Peshwa), while the shrine itself has ancient origins rooted in Hindu mythology.
history.architecture: Built in the Hemadpanthi style using black basalt stone, the temple features intricate carvings, majestic gateways, and a sacred sanctum surrounded by the Brahmagiri Hills.
history.importance: The temple is closely associated with Sage Gautama and is believed to be the origin of the sacred Godavari River. It is also one of the few temples where the Jyotirlinga represents the Hindu Trinity.

story: Welcome to the divine Trimbakeshwar Temple, one of the holiest Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva. Long ago, Sage Gautama lived peacefully on the Brahmagiri Hills with his wife Ahalya. Through his devotion, the land remained fertile even during a terrible famine, but jealous sages falsely accused him of committing a great sin. Deeply saddened, Gautama performed intense penance to seek Lord Shiva's forgiveness. Moved by his unwavering devotion, Lord Shiva appeared before him and released the sacred River Godavari from the Brahmagiri Hills to purify the world. At the sage's request, Lord Shiva chose to remain here forever as Trimbakeshwar, the unique Jyotirlinga representing Brahma, Vishnu, and Mahesh in one divine form. Even today, devotees believe that worshipping here washes away past sins and brings peace, wisdom, and spiritual liberation. May Lord Trimbakeshwar bless you and your family with health, prosperity, and divine grace.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. October to March offers pleasant weather, while Shravan and Maha Shivaratri attract huge crowds.

kakad_aarti.description: The temple opens with the sacred Kakad Aarti.

rudrabhishek.description: Special Rudrabhishek performed for Lord Shiva.

sayam_aarti.description: Evening aarti with Vedic chants and lamps.

shej_aarti.description: The final aarti before the temple closes.

best_visit_time: Early morning between 5:30 AM and 8:00 AM or during October to March for a peaceful darshan.
entry_fee: General temple entry is free. Separate charges apply for special poojas such as Rudrabhishek and Narayan Nagbali.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Men performing special rituals inside the sanctum are generally required to wear a dhoti.

name: Mahakaleshwar Temple

history.overview: Mahakaleshwar Temple is one of the twelve sacred Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva and is among the most revered Shiva temples in India.
history.built_by: The temple has ancient origins mentioned in the Shiva Purana. The present structure was rebuilt in the 18th century by the Maratha ruler Ranoji Shinde after earlier invasions damaged the shrine.
history.architecture: The temple showcases a blend of Bhumija, Maratha, and Chalukya architectural styles. It is a multi-level temple with the sanctum located underground and is famous for its magnificent Mahakal Lok corridor.
history.importance: Mahakaleshwar is the only south-facing (Dakshinamukhi) Jyotirlinga and is renowned worldwide for the unique Bhasma Aarti performed every morning.

story: Welcome to the sacred Mahakaleshwar Temple, where Lord Shiva is worshipped as Mahakal, the Lord who conquers time itself. Long ago, the peaceful city of Ujjain was terrorized by a powerful demon named Dushan, who disturbed sages and devotees performing their prayers. Unable to bear the suffering of His devotees, Lord Shiva emerged from the earth in His fierce Mahakal form with a thunderous roar. A great battle followed, and within moments, the mighty demon was defeated. The grateful sages prayed to Lord Shiva to remain here forever and protect the city. Accepting their devotion, He manifested as the sacred Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga. Even today, thousands gather before sunrise to witness the famous Bhasma Aarti, a unique ritual that reminds us that time spares no one, but true devotion is eternal. May Lord Mahakal remove every fear from your life and bless you with strength, peace, and spiritual wisdom.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. Maha Shivaratri and the Shravan month witness exceptionally large crowds.

bhasma_aarti.description: The world-famous Bhasma Aarti performed before sunrise. Advance booking is required.

dadyodak_aarti.description: Morning Bal Bhog Aarti offered to Lord Mahakal.

bhog_aarti.description: Midday Bhog Aarti with special offerings.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening Aarti with devotional chants and lamps.

shayan_aarti.description: The final Aarti before the temple closes.

best_visit_time: Early morning between 4:00 AM and 7:00 AM for Bhasma Aarti or October to March for pleasant weather.
entry_fee: General entry is free. Advance booking is required for Bhasma Aarti and special darshan services.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Men attending the Bhasma Aarti inside the sanctum are required to wear a dhoti as per temple rules.

name: Virupaksha Temple

history.overview: Virupaksha Temple is one of the oldest continuously functioning temples dedicated to Lord Shiva and is the most important shrine in the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Hampi.
history.built_by: The temple originated as a small shrine in the 7th century CE and was greatly expanded during the Vijayanagara Empire, particularly under Deva Raya II and Emperor Krishnadevaraya.
history.architecture: Built in the magnificent Vijayanagara style, the temple features a towering nine-storey eastern Gopuram, beautifully carved pillared halls, intricate ceiling paintings, and numerous smaller shrines.
history.importance: The temple is dedicated to Lord Virupaksha, a form of Lord Shiva, and is closely associated with Goddess Pampa. It is one of the very few temples in Hampi where worship has continued uninterrupted for over 1,300 years.

story: Welcome to the sacred Virupaksha Temple, the spiritual heart of Hampi. Have you ever wondered why this ancient temple has remained alive for more than thirteen centuries? According to legend, Goddess Pampa performed years of deep penance on the Hemakuta Hills, praying to marry Lord Shiva. Touched by her unwavering devotion, Lord Shiva appeared as Virupaksha and accepted her as His divine consort. Their celestial wedding took place on this sacred land, making Hampi a place of eternal love and devotion. Centuries later, mighty kings of the Vijayanagara Empire built this magnificent temple in their honor. Even when the great city of Hampi was destroyed in the 16th century, this temple survived, and prayers have never stopped. As you walk through these ancient halls, you are following the footsteps of saints, kings, and countless devotees. May Lord Virupaksha and Goddess Pampa bless your life with love, strength, peace, and everlasting prosperity.

infobox.UNESCO Status: Part of the Group of Monuments at Hampi

seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. October to February is the best time to visit due to pleasant weather.

entry_fee: Temple entry is free. Separate charges apply for camera usage and special poojas.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Footwear must be removed before entering the temple.

name: Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple

history.overview: Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple is one of the most famous and richest Hindu temples in the world, dedicated to Lord Vishnu in the form of Anantha Shayana (reclining posture on the serpent Ananta).
history.built_by: The temple has ancient origins, with references dating back to the 8th century. It was later rebuilt and expanded by the Travancore royal family in the 18th century.
history.architecture: The temple showcases a blend of Kerala and Dravidian architectural styles with a massive gopuram, intricate stone carvings, and a sacred seven-tiered entrance tower.
history.importance: The temple is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Lord Vishnu and is globally known for its immense spiritual significance and historic treasure vaults.

story: Welcome to the divine Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple, where Lord Vishnu rests in eternal peace on the serpent Ananta. According to legend, the sage Divakara Muni performed intense penance to see Lord Vishnu. Pleased by his devotion, the Lord appeared as a small child. The sage, overwhelmed with divine love, requested Him to stay forever in this land. Lord Vishnu then revealed His grand form and merged into the sacred Ananta Shayana posture, symbolizing cosmic balance and eternal protection of the universe. It is believed that the Travancore kings dedicated their kingdom to Lord Padmanabha and ruled as His servants. Even today, the temple is managed with divine discipline and deep devotion, making it one of the most sacred places in India.
seasonal_note: The temple is open throughout the year. October to March is the best time to visit Kerala due to pleasant weather.

nirmalya_darshanam.description: Early morning darshan of the deity adorned with previous night’s flowers.

athazha_pooja.description: Evening final pooja before temple closes.

best_visit_time: Early morning during Nirmalya Darshanam or evening aarti for peaceful spiritual experience.
entry_fee: Entry is free, but strict dress code is followed.
dress_code: Traditional attire is mandatory. Men must wear dhoti, and women must wear saree or modest traditional wear.

name: Mundeshwari Devi Temple

history.overview: Mundeshwari Devi Temple is one of the oldest continuously functioning Hindu temples in India, dedicated to Goddess Shakti (Mundeshwari) along with a Shiva lingam. It is located on the Mundeshwari Hills in the Kaimur region of Bihar.
history.built_by: The temple is believed to date back to the early centuries of the Common Era, with some archaeological estimates placing its origin around the 2nd century CE. It was constructed under ancient Nagara-style temple architecture, though exact builders remain unknown.
history.location: Mundeshwari Hills, Kaimur district, Bihar, India.
history.architecture: The temple is built in an octagonal plan using stone, showcasing early Nagara-style architecture. It contains intricate carvings and a unique sanctum housing both the Goddess and a Shiva lingam.
history.importance: The temple is considered one of the oldest functional Hindu temples in the world and holds immense religious importance for Shakti and Shiva worshippers.

story: According to local legends, Goddess Mundeshwari manifested on the hill to destroy demons and protect devotees. It is believed that the temple marks the place where divine energy of Shakti resides eternally. Devotees say that prayers offered here remove obstacles and bring protection, prosperity, and spiritual strength. The temple has remained continuously active for worship for many centuries, making it a living symbol of ancient Indian spirituality.
seasonal_note: The temple can be visited throughout the year. October to March is most comfortable due to pleasant weather in Bihar.

mangala_aarti.description: Early morning aarti and first darshan of the deity.

midday_pooja.description: Daily worship and offerings to Goddess Mundeshwari and Lord Shiva.

evening_aarti.description: Evening prayers and final darshan before closing.

best_visit_time: Early morning or during Navratri festivals for a deeply spiritual experience.
entry_fee: Entry is free for all devotees.
dress_code: Modest traditional attire is recommended to maintain temple decorum.

data_sources.history: Archaeological Survey of India reports and historical studies

name: Swaminarayan Akshardham Temple

history.overview: Swaminarayan Akshardham Temple is a modern Hindu temple complex dedicated to Bhagwan Swaminarayan, showcasing Indian art, culture, spirituality, and architecture. It is one of the largest Hindu temple complexes in the world.
history.built_by: The temple was built under the inspiration of Pramukh Swami Maharaj and inaugurated in 2005 by Bochasanwasi Akshar Purushottam Swaminarayan Sanstha (BAPS).
history.location: Near the banks of the Yamuna River, New Delhi, India.
history.architecture: The temple is built in traditional Nagara-style architecture using pink sandstone and white marble. It features intricate carvings of deities, flora, fauna, dancers, and musicians.
history.importance: Akshardham is a major cultural and spiritual complex attracting millions of visitors, symbolizing Indian heritage, devotion, and peace.

story: Akshardham represents the vision of divine peace and harmony inspired by Bhagwan Swaminarayan. It is believed that devotion, righteousness, and service lead to spiritual elevation. The temple complex narrates stories from ancient Indian scriptures and the life of Swaminarayan through exhibitions, boat rides, and light shows, guiding visitors on a journey of moral and spiritual learning.
seasonal_note: The temple is open throughout the year except Mondays. Winter months (October to March) are best for visiting due to pleasant weather in Delhi.

evening_sandhya_aarti.description: Evening devotional aarti performed at the main temple mandir.

abhishek_mandap.description: Ritual bathing ceremony of the sacred idol performed throughout the day.

best_visit_time: Early morning or evening during light and sound show for a complete cultural and spiritual experience.
entry_fee: Entry to temple complex is free; exhibitions and boat ride have ticket charges.
dress_code: Modest and traditional clothing is recommended. Shorts and sleeveless clothing are discouraged.

name: Shri Naina Devi Temple

history.overview: Shri Naina Devi Temple is one of the most revered Shakti Peethas in India. Situated atop the Shivalik Hills, the temple is dedicated to Goddess Naina Devi, an incarnation of Goddess Shakti.
history.built_by: According to tradition, the temple was established by Raja Bir Chand of Bilaspur in the 8th century. It has undergone several renovations over the centuries.
history.architecture: The temple showcases traditional North Indian temple architecture with beautifully decorated entrances, marble courtyards, and panoramic views of the Gobind Sagar Lake and surrounding hills.
history.importance: Naina Devi Temple is one of the 51 Shakti Peethas. It is believed that the eyes (Naina) of Goddess Sati fell at this sacred place, making it one of the holiest pilgrimage sites dedicated to Goddess Shakti.

story: Welcome to the sacred Shri Naina Devi Temple. According to Hindu mythology, after Goddess Sati immolated herself at her father Daksha's yajna, Lord Shiva carried her body across the universe in deep sorrow. To restore cosmic balance, Lord Vishnu used his Sudarshana Chakra to divide Sati's body into several parts. It is believed that the eyes (Naina) of Goddess Sati fell at this holy site, giving the temple its name, Naina Devi. Today, millions of devotees visit this sacred shrine to seek the blessings of the Divine Mother for protection, prosperity, courage, and spiritual well-being.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. Navratri festivals and the annual Shravan Ashtami Fair attract lakhs of devotees from across India.

mangala_aarti.description: The temple opens with the Mangala Aarti dedicated to Goddess Naina Devi.

morning_pooja.description: Morning worship with Vedic chants, bhajans, and offerings.

bhog_aarti.description: Midday Bhog is offered to Goddess Naina Devi followed by special prayers.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening Aarti is performed with lamps, devotional songs, and bells.

shayan_aarti.description: The final worship of the day is performed before the temple closes.

best_visit_time: March to June and September to November. Early morning (6:00 AM–9:00 AM) or evening (5:00 PM–7:30 PM) offer the best spiritual experience.
entry_fee: Entry is free for all devotees. Ropeway charges are applicable for visitors using the cable car service.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Visitors should dress respectfully while entering the temple premises.

name: Mahabodhi Temple

history.overview: The Mahabodhi Temple is one of the most sacred Buddhist pilgrimage sites in the world. It marks the place where Prince Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment under the Bodhi Tree and became the Buddha.
history.built_by: The original shrine was established by Emperor Ashoka in the 3rd century BCE. The present temple structure dates mainly from the 5th–6th century CE, with later restorations carried out over the centuries.
history.architecture: Built in classical brick architecture, the temple features a magnificent pyramidal tower rising about 55 meters high. The complex includes the sacred Bodhi Tree, Vajrasana (Diamond Throne), meditation gardens, and several ancient stupas.
history.importance: Mahabodhi Temple is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and the holiest pilgrimage destination in Buddhism. Millions of devotees from around the world visit the temple to meditate and pay homage to Lord Buddha.

story: Welcome to the sacred Mahabodhi Temple. More than 2,500 years ago, Prince Siddhartha Gautama meditated beneath the Bodhi Tree at this very place in search of the ultimate truth. After forty-nine days of deep meditation, he attained enlightenment and became the Buddha, the Enlightened One. This sacred site has since become the spiritual heart of Buddhism. Pilgrims from every corner of the world come here to meditate, offer prayers, and experience the profound peace associated with the place where the Buddha attained supreme wisdom. The Mahabodhi Temple stands today as a timeless symbol of compassion, wisdom, and enlightenment.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. The best time to visit is from October to March. Buddha Purnima attracts thousands of pilgrims from around the world.

morning_prayers.description: Morning prayers and meditation begin in the temple complex.

chanting_session.description: Monks and devotees perform Buddhist chanting and meditation.

evening_prayers.description: Evening prayer sessions and peaceful meditation are conducted.

night_meditation.description: Devotees gather for silent meditation before the temple closes.

best_visit_time: October to March. Early morning (5:30 AM–8:00 AM) and evening (5:00 PM–7:00 PM) provide a peaceful atmosphere for meditation.
entry_fee: Entry is free for all visitors.
dress_code: Visitors should wear modest and respectful attire. Silence and proper decorum are expected inside the temple complex.

name: Dakshineswar Kali Temple

history.overview: Dakshineswar Kali Temple is one of the most famous temples dedicated to Goddess Kali in India. Situated on the eastern bank of the Hooghly River, it is renowned for its spiritual significance and its close association with the saint Sri Ramakrishna Paramahamsa.
history.built_by: The temple was built in 1855 by Rani Rashmoni, a philanthropist and devotee of Goddess Kali.
history.architecture: Built in the traditional Navaratna style of Bengal architecture, the temple features a magnificent nine-spired shrine dedicated to Goddess Bhavatarini. The temple complex also includes twelve Shiva temples, a Radha-Krishna temple, spacious courtyards, and bathing ghats along the Hooghly River.
history.importance: Dakshineswar Kali Temple is one of the most revered Shakti temples in India. It is closely associated with Sri Ramakrishna Paramahamsa, whose teachings inspired the Ramakrishna Mission founded by Swami Vivekananda.

story: Welcome to the sacred Dakshineswar Kali Temple. According to tradition, Rani Rashmoni had a divine vision of Goddess Kali before embarking on a pilgrimage to Varanasi. Following the Goddess's command, she built this magnificent temple on the banks of the Hooghly River in 1855. The temple later became the spiritual home of Sri Ramakrishna Paramahamsa, whose intense devotion and mystical experiences transformed it into one of India's most important centers of spirituality. Devotees from around the world visit this holy shrine to seek the blessings of Goddess Bhavatarini, the compassionate Mother who liberates her devotees from worldly suffering.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. Kali Puja and Durga Puja are the busiest festivals, attracting thousands of devotees.

mangala_aarti.description: The temple opens with the Mangala Aarti and morning worship of Goddess Bhavatarini.

morning_pooja.description: Morning rituals, devotional hymns, and offerings are performed.

bhog_aarti.description: Midday Bhog is offered to the Goddess followed by special prayers.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening Aarti is performed with lamps, bells, and devotional chants.

shayan_aarti.description: The final worship of the day is performed before the temple closes.

best_visit_time: October to March offers pleasant weather. Early morning (6:00 AM–8:00 AM) and evening (5:30 PM–7:30 PM) provide the best spiritual experience.
entry_fee: Entry is free for all devotees.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. Visitors should dress respectfully while entering the temple premises.

name: Golden Temple (Sri Harmandir Sahib)

history.overview: The Golden Temple, officially known as Sri Harmandir Sahib, is the holiest Gurdwara in Sikhism. It is a symbol of equality, peace, humility, and universal brotherhood, welcoming people of all religions and backgrounds.
history.built_by: The foundation stone was laid in 1581 by Guru Arjan Dev Ji, the fifth Sikh Guru. The shrine was completed in 1604, and Maharaja Ranjit Singh later covered the upper structure with gold during the early 19th century.
history.architecture: The Golden Temple combines Sikh and Mughal architectural styles. Its gold-covered sanctum is surrounded by the sacred Amrit Sarovar (Pool of Nectar), with a marble pathway leading to the shrine.
history.importance: Sri Harmandir Sahib is the most sacred pilgrimage site in Sikhism. It represents equality, selfless service (Seva), devotion, and spiritual unity. The Gurdwara is also famous for serving free meals (Langar) to thousands of visitors every day.

story: Welcome to the Golden Temple, Sri Harmandir Sahib. Guru Arjan Dev Ji envisioned this sacred place as a spiritual center open to all people, regardless of caste, religion, or background. The Guru Granth Sahib, the eternal Guru of Sikhism, was first installed here in 1604. The temple's four entrances symbolize openness and equality for humanity. Every day, thousands of devotees gather to offer prayers, listen to Gurbani Kirtan, and participate in the community Langar, which reflects the Sikh principles of selfless service and unity. The shimmering golden shrine and the peaceful Amrit Sarovar inspire visitors from around the world with a message of faith, compassion, and harmony.
seasonal_note: The Golden Temple remains open throughout the year. Festivals such as Baisakhi and Guru Nanak Gurpurab attract lakhs of devotees from across the world.

prakash_sewa.description: The Guru Granth Sahib is ceremonially brought into the sanctum in the early morning.

gurbani_kirtan.description: Continuous devotional singing of Gurbani (Kirtan) is performed throughout the day.

langar_seva.description: The community kitchen (Langar) serves free meals to all visitors without discrimination.

sukhasan_sewa.description: The Guru Granth Sahib is respectfully taken to the Akal Takht in the nightly Sukhasan ceremony.

best_visit_time: October to March offers pleasant weather. Early morning and late evening are especially peaceful, and the illuminated shrine at night is a remarkable sight.
entry_fee: Entry is free for all visitors.
dress_code: Visitors must cover their heads, remove their shoes, dress modestly, and maintain respect inside the Gurdwara premises.
