name: Baba Baidyanath Temple

history.overview: Baba Baidyanath Temple, also known as Baidyanath Dham, is one of the twelve sacred Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva. It is one of the most revered pilgrimage destinations for Hindus and attracts millions of devotees every year.
history.built_by: The temple is believed to have ancient origins. According to tradition, the present temple complex has been maintained and renovated by various regional rulers over the centuries.
history.architecture: The temple is built in the traditional Nagara style with a towering white shikhara. The temple complex consists of the main Jyotirlinga shrine and 21 surrounding temples dedicated to various Hindu deities.
history.importance: Baidyanath Temple is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas and one of the 51 Shakti Peethas. It holds immense religious significance for devotees of both Lord Shiva and Goddess Shakti.

story: Welcome to the sacred Baba Baidyanath Temple. According to Hindu mythology, Ravana performed intense penance to please Lord Shiva and offered his ten heads one by one. Impressed by his devotion, Lord Shiva restored Ravana's heads and became known as 'Vaidyanath,' meaning the Divine Physician. Lord Shiva then granted Ravana a Jyotirlinga with the condition that it should never be placed on the ground. During his journey to Lanka, Ravana temporarily handed the Jyotirlinga to Lord Ganesha, who appeared in the form of a young boy. Ganesha placed the Jyotirlinga on the ground at Deoghar, where it became permanently established. Since then, Baba Baidyanath Dham has been one of the holiest pilgrimage sites dedicated to Lord Shiva.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. The largest gathering of devotees takes place during the Shravani Mela (July–August), when millions of Kanwariyas visit the shrine.

mangala_aarti.description: The temple opens with Mangala Aarti and the sacred Abhishek of Baba Baidyanath.

morning_pooja.description: Morning worship with Vedic chants, Rudrabhishek, and offerings to the Jyotirlinga.

afternoon_pooja.description: Special prayers and offerings are performed during midday worship.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening Aarti is performed with lamps, bells, and devotional hymns.

shayan_aarti.description: The final Aarti of the day is performed before the temple closes.

best_visit_time: October to March for pleasant weather. Devotees seeking the grand spiritual experience often visit during the Shravani Mela in July and August.
entry_fee: General temple entry is free. Charges may apply for special pujas and VIP darshan.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended. During the Shravani Mela, devotees generally wear saffron-colored clothing while carrying holy water for Jalabhishek.

name: Swaminarayan Akshardham

history.overview: Swaminarayan Akshardham is one of India's most magnificent Hindu temple complexes, dedicated to Bhagwan Swaminarayan. The temple showcases India's rich spiritual heritage, culture, architecture, and timeless values through intricate carvings, exhibitions, gardens, and cultural displays.
history.built_by: Constructed by BAPS Swaminarayan Sanstha under the guidance of His Holiness Pramukh Swami Maharaj.
history.location: NH 24, Akshardham Setu, New Delhi, Delhi.
history.architecture: Built in traditional Nagara style using pink sandstone from Rajasthan and white marble from Italy. The monument features over 20,000 intricately carved figures of deities, saints, musicians, animals, and floral motifs. No steel or reinforced concrete has been used in the main monument.
history.importance: Akshardham is a symbol of Indian spirituality, devotion, culture, and craftsmanship. It attracts millions of visitors every year and serves as a center for worship, learning, and cultural inspiration.

story: Welcome to Swaminarayan Akshardham. The temple is dedicated to Bhagwan Swaminarayan, who inspired millions through his teachings of devotion, compassion, righteousness, and selfless service. The monument was built to preserve India's spiritual heritage and showcase the country's ancient traditions through breathtaking architecture and immersive exhibitions. Visitors experience inspiring displays on Indian culture, the values taught by Bhagwan Swaminarayan, and the timeless wisdom of Sanatan Dharma. The peaceful atmosphere, musical fountain, and beautifully landscaped gardens make Akshardham one of the most visited spiritual destinations in India.
seasonal_note: The temple is closed every Monday. October to March is considered the best season due to pleasant weather. Evening visits are especially popular for the Sahaj Anand Water Show.

morning_aarti.description: Morning prayers and devotional worship are offered inside the temple.

rajbhog_darshan.description: Midday darshan and offerings are presented to Bhagwan Swaminarayan.

evening_aarti.description: Evening Aarti is performed with devotional hymns and lamps.

water_show.description: The famous Sahaj Anand Water Show presents stories from the Upanishads using water, lights, lasers, and music.

best_visit_time: October to March. Visit between 10:00 AM–1:00 PM for comfortable sightseeing or after 4:00 PM to enjoy the evening atmosphere and Water Show.
entry_fee: Temple entry is free. Separate tickets are required for exhibitions and the Sahaj Anand Water Show.
dress_code: Visitors should wear modest, traditional, or respectful attire covering shoulders and knees. Sleeveless tops, shorts, and revealing clothing are not permitted.

name: Baijnath Temple

history.overview: Baijnath Temple is one of the oldest and most revered Shiva temples in North India. Dedicated to Lord Shiva in his form as Vaidyanath, the temple is renowned for its remarkable Nagara-style architecture and exquisite stone carvings.
history.built_by: The temple was constructed in 1204 CE by two local merchants, Ahuka and Manyuka.
history.architecture: Built in the Nagara style, the temple features a magnificent stone shikhara, beautifully carved pillars, intricate sculptures, and a sanctum housing the sacred Shiva Lingam. It is protected as a Monument of National Importance by the Archaeological Survey of India.
history.importance: Baijnath Temple is one of the most significant Shiva temples in Himachal Pradesh and is regarded as a sacred place where devotees seek blessings for health, healing, prosperity, and spiritual well-being.

story: Welcome to the sacred Baijnath Temple. According to Hindu tradition, Ravana performed intense penance to please Lord Shiva and received the divine Shiva Lingam from Him. While carrying it towards Lanka, Ravana was unable to continue and placed the Lingam on the ground. Once placed, it became permanently established at Baijnath. Lord Shiva is worshipped here as Vaidyanath, the Divine Physician, believed to bless devotees with healing, health, and protection. Surrounded by the beautiful Dhauladhar mountain range, the temple offers a peaceful spiritual atmosphere and has attracted pilgrims for centuries.
seasonal_note: The temple remains open throughout the year. The most pleasant months to visit are October to March, while Maha Shivaratri attracts thousands of devotees.

mangala_aarti.description: The temple opens with the Mangala Aarti and Abhishek of Lord Shiva.

morning_pooja.description: Morning worship with Vedic chants, Rudrabhishek, and offerings.

afternoon_pooja.description: Midday prayers and special offerings are presented to Lord Vaidyanath.

sandhya_aarti.description: Evening Aarti is performed with lamps, bells, and devotional hymns.

best_visit_time: October to March. Early morning (6:00 AM–9:00 AM) and evening (5:00 PM–7:30 PM) are ideal for a peaceful darshan.
entry_fee: Entry is free for all devotees.
dress_code: Traditional or modest attire is recommended while visiting the temple.
